mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) indicates a robust and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and, consequently, positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer investment horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (mean return not statistically different from zero) during periods of sustained GDPC1 growth above its historical average.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a specific threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, which negatively impacts future S&P 500 returns by compressing valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:higher rates increase cost of capital
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a tight and healthy labor market. This environment supports robust consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where both PAYEMS growth and UNRATE conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity and business investment. This strength in the supply side of the economy translates to positive S&P 500 returns, but this effect is conditional on the broader economic regime.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified economic expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand. This directly impacts corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods following a significant decline in RSAFS as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power for consumers and increases input costs for businesses. This leads to lower corporate profitability and, consequently, negative S&P 500 returns over a longer investment horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat (mean return not statistically different from zero) during periods of sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 3% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness. This leads to reduced consumer confidence and spending, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns due to lower earnings forecasts.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where the unemployment rate rises sharply according to the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong housing starts (HOUST) and a relatively low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a healthy housing market supported by accommodative monetary conditions. This typically boosts broader economic activity and leads to short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market is a key economic driver
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both housing starts are strong and FEDFUNDS is low as per the trigger conditions.
